goal: Scan inbox emails from the last 7 days for complaint-related messages and append new matching emails to the specified spreadsheet tab while skipping rows already logged by Gmail message link/id.

config.description: Destination spreadsheet identifier for logging complaint emails.
Destination sheet tab name for logged complaint emails.

config.default: in:inbox newer_than:7d (complaint OR refund OR angry OR "not working")
config.description: Gmail search query for inbox messages in scope.
Complaint keywords used for case-insensitive matching against email content.

config.default: skip if Gmail message link/id already exists in the sheet
config.description: Deduplication rule for avoiding duplicate sheet rows.

config.default: log every message that matches the complaint rule
config.description: Thread handling rule for logging matching messages independently.

config.default: sender email, subject, date, full email text, Gmail message link/id
config.description: Expected destination column order in the sheet tab.
User email address used to construct Gmail message links.

invariants.description: Only messages matching the complaint rule are eligible for logging.
Each matching Gmail message is treated independently, even within the same thread.
Do not append a row when the Gmail message link/id already exists in the destination tab.
Appended rows must preserve the exact column order: sender email, subject, date, full email text, Gmail message link/id.

required_outcomes: Fetch inbox emails from the last 7 days with full message content.
Read existing rows from the destination sheet tab.
Identify complaint emails using case-insensitive keyword matching on email content.
Append exactly one row for each new matching message not already present in the sheet.

steps.summary: Search inbox emails from the last 7 days with full content for complaint candidates.
Read existing rows from the destination sheet tab to identify already logged Gmail message link/id values.
Extract the existing Gmail message link/id values from the destination sheet rows for deduplication.

generate.instruction: From the sheet row data, extract the values from the fifth column only and return them as a normalized list of existing Gmail message link/id values. Ignore header rows or blank rows.

outputs.name: message_ids
outputs.description: Existing Gmail message link/id values already present in the sheet.

steps.summary: Identify complaint emails, construct Gmail message link/id values, and prepare rows in the required column order.

generate.instruction: Review each email independently. Treat an email as a complaint if its subject, snippet, or body contains any of these keywords in a case-insensitive way: complaint, refund, angry, not working. For each matching email, produce one output record with these fields: sender_email from the email field 'from', subject from 'subject', date from 'date', full_email_text from 'body', gmail_message_link_id constructed as a Gmail message URL using the user's email context and the email field 'id', row_values as an array in this exact order: sender email, subject, date, full email text, Gmail message link/id. Include the original email id as id. Exclude non-matching emails. Treat every matching message independently even if multiple messages belong to the same thread.

outputs.name: rows
outputs.description: Prepared complaint email records ready for deduplication and append.

steps.summary: Remove complaint email rows whose Gmail message link/id already exists in the destination sheet.

generate.instruction: Compare each prepared complaint email record against the existing Gmail message link/id list. Keep only records whose gmail_message_link_id is not already present. Return the remaining records unchanged and also return a 2D array of row values ready for sheet append. Apply the deduplication rule exactly as configured: skip if Gmail message link/id already exists in the sheet.

outputs.name: rows
outputs.description: New complaint email records not yet logged.

outputs.name: values
outputs.description: 2D array of row values ready to append to the sheet.

steps.summary: Append new complaint email rows to the destination sheet tab.

meta.plan_title: Customer Complaint Email Logger (Gmail → Google Sheets)
